  "findings": [
    "Backstabbed device termination used on a 20-ampere branch circuit."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pigtail the conductors and terminate them using the side-screw terminals or pressure plates rated for 20A.",
    "Verify all other receptacles on the 20A circuit are not backstabbed."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under UL listing requirements and NEC 110.14(B), standard push-in (backstabbed) terminations on typical receptacles are only listed and rated for 15A circuits using solid 14 AWG copper wire. Using backstabbed connections on a 20A circuit (which requires 12 AWG wire) is a direct violation of the device's listing and manufacturer instructions, making it non-compliant with NEC 110.3(B) and 110.14.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "UL 498"
  ]
}
